prompts.text: Vocabulary to use throughout: 'left', 'right', 'up', 'down', 'towards', 'away from', 'around', 'past'. Point in the direction as you say the word every time — the gesture reinforces the language.
Model thinking-aloud route planning: 'I can see the target is to the right, but there's a block in the way — I'll have to go around it.' Narrate the reasoning, not just the moves.
Core question to return to: 'Which direction does the block need to go first? What's in the way?' — slows down impulse tapping and builds planning habit.
Resilience framing to give before starting: 'If we get stuck, that's fine — we back up and try a different way. That's what problem-solving looks like.'
